calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Apr-07-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT STEPHEN TODD COLLETTI'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Stephen Colletti's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to all three causes of action alleged against him by plaintiff Accelerated Inventory Management, LLC is denied as to all three causes of action. The motion is premised on the novel argument that a complaint filed by an attorney or law firm engaged in debt collection who is not licensed as a debt collector forever bars the plaintiff from seeking court relief as to the plaintiff's claim for a debt owed to it that is the subject of the complaint. . No statute, rule or case law supports Colletti's argument. Colletti relies on case law that renders an unlicensed plaintiff's actions void. The general rule is that a party to a lawsuit and the person who purports to represent that party are separate legal persons and conduct that bars the person who purports to represent the party from representing the party does not preclude the party from seeking relief in court either when its lawyer becomes eligible to represent the party, when the party obtains alternate representation or, if the party is a human, if the the party choose to represents herself. (See, e.g. CCP 286 (when an attorney who represents a litigant is not able to represent that litigant in a court case, the litigant must be given an opportunity to obtain an attorney who is able to represent the litigant prior to any adverse action be taken against the party); CLD Construction, Inc. v. City of San Ramon (2004) 120 Cal. App. 4th 1141 (error to dismiss a lawsuit filed by a corporation when the corporation was not represented by an attorney since the failure of the corporation to be represented by an attorney was a curable defect); Aldrich v. San Fernando Valley Lumber Co, Inc. (1985) 170 Cal. App. 3d 725 (defendant who was represented by a suspended attorney was entitled to be given an opportunity to obtain alternate counsel)). The purpose of the rule is not to oust a party from court merely because its attorney/legal representative is not able to represent the party in court. There is no reason why this rule should not apply to this motion. Since Colletti acknowledges that Accelerated's counsel is now licensed as a debt collector, Coilleti's motion is denied. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)
